signals.idea: Implement structured editable live-update documents for the TopicEditor. Current behavior: post_sectioned_topic stores a body plus loose sections and topic-level source_message_ids; render_topic emits one big Discord message with a Sources footer of raw message IDs; section media is dropped or model-copied into simple-topic media; publisher sends a single rendered string and trims at 2000 chars. Desired behavior: the agent creates a document-like draft in topics.summary with ordered blocks/sections. Each intro/section block carries its own source_message_ids and media_refs. Source citations render inline in brackets at the relevant block, not as an overall footer. Media refs bind by stable reference such as message_id plus attachment_index, not copied CDN URLs. Publisher resolves source IDs to Discord jump URLs using message metadata and resolves media refs to attachment/embed URLs at publish time. Publish order should be deterministic: header, intro text with inline linked sources, intro media, each section text with inline linked sources, that section media, one block at a time. Text chunking should be paragraph-aware only when a block exceeds Discord limits; normal sectioned topics should send one section per message. Add validation so media_refs must resolve to source messages/attachments and unknown refs do not silently disappear. Preserve existing topic_sources storage as the union of all block-level source IDs for audit/search. Update prompts/tool schemas so the agent is conscientious: every factual block gets its own sources; media is attached only to the relevant block; no global source footer. Keep backwards compatibility for old summaries with body/sections/source_message_ids as much as reasonable. Add focused tests covering section-level sources, media refs sent after relevant sections, no footer, source link hydration, chunking, and existing simple topic behavior.

unresolved_flags.concern: TopicEditor source resolution: archive-resolved block sources must feed source_authors and collision checks, not only validation/publishing hydration.
Media ref discovery: archive `get_message_context` output lacks attachments/embeds, so agents may not have enough metadata to produce stable media_refs for messages found through read tools.
Media ref schema: attachment-only `{message_id, attachment_index}` does not define how embed URLs are addressed despite the requirement to resolve attachment/embed URLs.
Structured blocks acceptance: existing `post_sectioned_topic` rejection on missing `sections` must be relaxed for valid `blocks` payloads.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Issue hints require media refs to resolve to attachment/embed URLs at publish time, but the plan leaves embed addressing unresolved in its questions and assumptions while still saying validation should resolve attachments/embeds. A schema with only `{message_id, attachment_index}` can address attachments, but it cannot unambiguously address embeds unless the implementation defines a combined namespace or adds a `{kind, index}` field.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked `_dispatch_create_topic_tool`: it currently derives `source_messages` and `source_authors` only from `context["messages"]` via `_messages_by_id` before collision detection and post_simple validation. The plan says block sources may be accepted when resolved by DB lookup outside the current source window, but it does not explicitly require the resolved DB rows to feed `source_authors`, so simple-topic author limits and collision author-overlap checks could become wrong for archive-sourced blocks.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Searched TopicEditor media handling and found existing `_message_payload`, `_filter_source_messages`, `_dispatch_understand_media_tool`, and `_enrich_media_understandings` paths already work with `attachments`/`embeds` or attachment indices. The plan updates publishing hydration, but should also preserve or reuse these existing source-window media conventions so new `media_refs` do not diverge from the current `understand_media` tool's `{message_id, attachment_index}` contract.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Checked `storage_handler.get_topic_editor_message_context`: it selects only `message_id,channel_id,author_id,content,created_at,reference_id,thread_id`, so archive/read-tool messages currently do not include `guild_id`, `attachments`, or `embeds`. The plan adds a publishing lookup with those fields, but it does not explicitly update the `get_message_context` read tool output that the agent would use to discover exact attachment indices for archive messages.

Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked `_summary_for_tool` callers through `_dispatch_create_topic_tool`: all create tools currently pass raw tool args, and `post_sectioned_topic` currently rejects calls without `sections`. The plan says new `blocks` inputs should be accepted, but the dispatch rejection condition must be changed from `not args.get("sections")` to allow valid `blocks`; otherwise new structured calls would be rejected before normalization.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 12.5. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 9.

items.criterion: Structured `post_sectioned_topic` inputs store an ordered document in `topics.summary` with block-level `source_message_ids` and `media_refs`.
The persisted topic-level `source_message_ids` and `topic_sources` rows equal the distinct union of all block-level source IDs.
Structured document publishing renders inline linked source citations at each relevant block and does not append a global Sources footer.
Publisher resolves Discord jump URLs from message metadata at publish time using `guild_id`, `channel_id`, and `message_id`.
Publisher resolves media refs by stable source references and sends media immediately after the block that owns the media.
Invalid block source IDs or media refs are rejected with an auditable transition error rather than being silently omitted.
Normal structured sectioned topics send one Discord message per text block, and paragraph-aware chunking is used only for blocks that exceed Discord limits.
Existing simple-topic rendering and publishing behavior remains compatible unless the summary uses the new structured `blocks` shape.
Focused TopicEditor tests cover section-level sources, media ordering, no footer, source hydration, chunking, validation failures, and simple-topic compatibility.
The implementation keeps normalization, validation, rendering, and Discord side effects separated into small helpers rather than embedding all logic in `_publish_topic`.

unresolved_flags.concern: TopicEditor source resolution: archive-resolved block sources must feed source_authors and collision checks, not only validation/publishing hydration.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `_dispatch_create_topic_tool` currently computes `source_messages = self._messages_by_id(context["messages"], source_ids)` and `source_authors = self._source_authors(source_messages)` before post_simple validation and `detect_topic_collisions`; the plan allows source IDs outside the source window but does not state that DB-resolved messages replace/augment this author set.

unresolved_flags.concern: Media ref discovery: archive `get_message_context` output lacks attachments/embeds, so agents may not have enough metadata to produce stable media_refs for messages found through read tools.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `storage_handler.get_topic_editor_message_context` selects `message_id,channel_id,author_id,content,created_at,reference_id,thread_id` only. The plan adds a publish-time lookup but does not explicitly enrich this read-tool response with attachment/embed counts or ref-addressable media metadata.

unresolved_flags.concern: Media ref schema: attachment-only `{message_id, attachment_index}` does not define how embed URLs are addressed despite the requirement to resolve attachment/embed URLs.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The plan asks whether embeds should share the attachment index namespace or use `{kind, index}`, while Step 3 says every `media_ref` has `message_id` and integer `attachment_index` and Step 4 says lookup returns attachments and embeds. Without resolving that plan question, embed media cannot be validated or published deterministically.

unresolved_flags.concern: Structured blocks acceptance: existing `post_sectioned_topic` rejection on missing `sections` must be relaxed for valid `blocks` payloads.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `_dispatch_create_topic_tool` currently has `if call["name"] == "post_sectioned_topic" and not args.get("sections"):` returning `rejected_post_sectioned`. The plan's schema update allows `blocks`, but the execution steps do not call out this specific guard.

A schema with only `{message_id, attachment_index}` can address attachments, but it cannot unambiguously address embeds unless the implementation defines a combined namespace or adds a `{kind, index}` field.
